career_angle: Valuable only if you're deploying Qwen/Kimi/DeepSeek inference at scale. For most engineers, this is a niche ops problem that won't touch your work; read it to sound smart in ML rooms, not because you need to fix it.

dimensions.name: Source Authority
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks AI is a legitimate, well-funded inference optimization company with real customers and deployed infrastructure. Their engineers actually run these models in production.

dimensions.name: Evidence Quality
dimensions.rationale: Mentions specific models and optimization types (kernel fusions, all-reduce, routed experts), but the provided excerpt is truncated. Without the full post and code examples/benchmarks, it reads more narrative than evidence-driven.

dimensions.name: Reproducibility
dimensions.rationale: Hard to verify unless you control Kimi K2.5, Qwen3.5-MoE, and a custom serving stack. The numerical drift phenomenon itself is real and verifiable in principle, but inaccessible to most readers.

dimensions.name: Conflict of Interest
dimensions.rationale: Glaring conflict. This is a case study in why Fireworks' platform is necessary — they found these bugs, fixed them, now readers should care about their solution. Pure product positioning masquerading as technical education.

dimensions.name: Consensus
dimensions.rationale: MoE numerical parity is a real, acknowledged problem in the ML community (see DeepSeek V3 papers, Qwen research). However, it's niche expertise—only practitioners at scale deal with it. Not mainstream consensus, but not fringe either.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.rationale: Extremely specific. Names actual architectures (Kimi K2.5, Qwen3.5-MoE, DeepSeek V3), optimization techniques (kernel fusions, shared expert pathways, all-reduce), and failure modes (RLHF/GRPO reward exploitation). Claims are falsifiable.

dimensions.name: Track Record
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks has credibility—legitimate infrastructure, real customers, engineers who've shipped serving layers. However, they're also selling a product, so this piece is inherently biased toward their interests.
